@[reducible, simp]
def SwapBitvectors_postcond (X : UInt8) (Y : UInt8) (result: UInt8 × UInt8) (h_precond : SwapBitvectors_precond (X) (Y)) :=

theorem SwapBitvectors_spec_satisfied (X: UInt8) (Y: UInt8) (h_precond : SwapBitvectors_precond (X) (Y)) :

    rw [← BitVec.xor_assoc, BitVec.xor_self, BitVec.zero_xor]

    · -- Prove result.snd = X
      -- newY = (X ^ Y) ^ Y
      rw [BitVec.xor_assoc, BitVec.xor_self, BitVec.xor_zero]
    · -- Prove X ≠ Y → result.fst ≠ X ∧ result.snd ≠ Y

  let res := b.foldl (fun acc x => if !inArray a x then acc.insert x else acc) res

-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def dissimilarElements_postcond (a : Array Int) (b : Array Int) (result: Array Int) (h_precond : dissimilarElements_precond (a) (b)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  result.all (fun x => inArray a x ≠ inArray b x)∧
  result.toList.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) ∧
  a.all (fun x => if x ∈ b then x ∉ result else x ∈ result) ∧
  b.all (fun x => if x ∈ a then x ∉ result else x ∈ result)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem dissimilarElements_spec_satisfied (a: Array Int) (b: Array Int) (h_precond : dissimilarElements_precond (a) (b)) :
    dissimilarElements_postcond (a) (b) (dissimilarElements (a) (b) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold dissimilarElements dissimilarElements_postcond inArray
  simp (config := { decide := true })
  
  -- Normalize membership tests
  have h_any_mem : ∀ (arr : Array Int) (x : Int), (arr.any (fun y => y = x)) = true ↔ x ∈ arr := by
    intro arr x
    simp [Array.any_eq_true, Array.mem_def]
    
  simp only [h_any_mem]
  
  -- Define the intermediate HashSet states
  let s1 := a.foldl (fun acc x => if x ∈ b then acc else Std.HashSet.insert acc x) Std.HashSet.empty
  let s2 := b.foldl (fun acc x => if x ∈ a then acc else Std.HashSet.insert acc x) s1
  
  -- Lemma 1: Property of the first fold
  have h_s1 : ∀ x, x ∈ s1 ↔ x ∈ a ∧ x ∉ b := by
    have h_foldl_toList : s1 = a.toList.foldl (fun acc x => if x ∈ b then acc else Std.HashSet.insert acc x) Std.HashSet.empty := by
      simp [s1]

    rw [h_foldl_toList]
    induction a.toList with
    | nil => 
      simp [List.foldl_nil, Std.HashSet.mem_empty_iff_false]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      simp only [List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      intro y
      specialize ih y
      by_cases hxb : x ∈ b

        constructor

          have h_ne : y ≠ x := by
            intro heq
            rw [heq] at hxb
            exact h_mem.2 hxb
          have h_mem_xs : y ∈ xs := by
            rw [ih] at h_mem
            exact h_mem.1.2
          exact ⟨Or.inr h_mem_xs, h_mem.2⟩
        · intro h_mem
          have h_mem_xs : y ∈ xs := h_mem.1.resolve_left (by intro; contradiction)
          have h_mem_s := ih.mpr ⟨h_mem_xs, h_mem.2⟩
          exact h_mem_s
      · simp only [if_neg hxb]
        rw [Std.HashSet.mem_insert_iff]
        constructor
        · intro h_or
          rcases h_or with rfl | h_mem
          · exact ⟨Or.inl rfl, hxb⟩
          · have h_mem_s := ih.mp h_mem
            exact ⟨Or.inr h_mem_s.1, h_mem_s.2⟩
        · intro h_mem
          rcases h_mem.1 with rfl | h_mem_xs
          · left; rfl
          · right
            exact ih.mpr ⟨h_mem_xs, h_mem.2⟩

  -- Lemma 2: Property of the second fold
  have h_s2 : ∀ x, x ∈ s2 ↔ (x ∈ a ∧ x ∉ b) ∨ (x ∈ b ∧ x ∉ a) := by
    have h_foldl_toList : s2 = b.toList.foldl (fun acc x => if x ∈ a then acc else Std.HashSet.insert acc x) s1 := by
      simp [s2]
      rfl
    rw [h_foldl_toList]
    induction b.toList with
    | nil => 
      simp only [List.foldl_nil]
      intro x
      rw [h_s1]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      simp only [List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      intro y
      specialize ih y
      by_cases hxa : x ∈ a
      · simp only [if_pos hxa]
        constructor
        · intro h_mem
          have h_ne : y ≠ x := by
            intro heq
            rw [heq] at hxa
            cases h_mem with
            | inl h_left => exact h_left.2.1 (Or.inl rfl)
            | inr h_right => exact h_right.2 hxa
          exact ih.mp h_mem
        · intro h_mem
          have h_mem_s1 := ih.mpr h_mem
          -- We need to know x was not inserted in this step, so membership is preserved
          have h_not_ins : ¬ Std.HashSet.insert s1 x = s1 := by simp [Std.HashSet.insert_ne_self]
          -- Focusing on logical condition:
          -- if y ∈ s1, then y ∈ s2 (since we didn't insert x).
          -- If y = x, then x ∈ a, so y ∈ a. y ∉ b if we want Or.inl.
          -- But x ∈ xs+1.
          -- Logic: y ∈ s2 ↔ y ∈ s1 (since x ∈ a prevents insertion of x).
          -- And y ∈ s1 ↔ (y ∈ a ∧ y ∉ b) (by h_s1).
          -- Does this cover y ∈ b ∧ y ∉ a? No.
          -- If y ∈ b (i.e. y = x or y ∈ xs), and y ∉ a.
          -- If y = x, x ∈ a contradicts y ∉ a. So y ≠ x.
          -- So y ∈ xs.
          -- If y ∈ b ∧ y ∉ a, then y ∈ s1? No, s1 excludes b.
          -- Wait. s1 contains (A \ B).
          -- s2 adds (B \ A).
          -- If y ∈ b ∧ y ∉ a:
          --   If y = x: x ∉ a is false by assumption hxa. Contradiction.
          --   If y ∈ xs: y is processed later?
          -- The fold processes x (head) then xs.
          -- If we don't insert x (because x ∈ a), s2 = s1 + (processed xs).
          -- But s1 already filtered out all b.
          -- So s1 has no elements from b.
          -- If we don't insert x, and y ∈ b, y must come from xs processing?
          -- But ih assumes accumulator is s1.
          -- ih : y ∈ acc_after_xs ↔ (y ∈ a ∧ y ∉ b) ∨ (y ∈ xs ∧ y ∉ a)
          -- Here acc_after_xs = foldl ... s1.
          -- Since we didn't insert x, current acc is s1.
          -- The result is foldl ... s1.
          -- So y ∈ s2 ↔ y ∈ s1 ∨ (y ∈ xs ∧ y ∉ a).
          -- y ∈ s1 ↔ y ∈ a ∧ y ∉ b.
          -- So y ∈ s2 ↔ (y ∈ a ∧ y ∉ b) ∨ (y ∈ xs ∧ y ∉ a).
          -- Since x ∈ a, x ∉ (xs ∧ y ∉ a).
          -- So this correctly handles the head.
          -- Correct logic: if x ∈ a, we skip insertion. Membership is determined by IH.
          -- IH covers the tail xs. The tail elements in b are b.tail?
          -- Wait. y ∈ b means y = x ∨ y ∈ xs.
          -- y ∈ s2 ↔ (y ∈ a ∧ y ∉ b) ∨ (y ∈ xs ∧ y ∉ a).
          -- Does this match (y ∈ a ∧ y ∉ b) ∨ (y ∈ b ∧ y ∉ a)?
          -- If y ∈ b ∧ y ∉ a:
          --   If y = x: impossible since x ∈ a.
          --   If y ∈ xs: matches (y ∈ xs ∧ y ∉ a).
          -- So it holds.
          exact ih.mp h_mem
          
      · simp only [if_neg hxa]
        rw [Std.HashSet.mem_insert_iff]
        constructor
        · intro h_or
          rcases h_or with rfl | h_mem
          · -- y = x
            right
            right
            constructor
            · left; rfl
            · exact hxa
          · -- y ∈ s1
            have h_mem_s1 := ih.mp h_mem
            rcases h_mem_s1 with ⟨ha, hb⟩ | ⟨hb, ha⟩
            · left; exact ⟨ha, hb⟩
            · right
              constructor
              · right; exact hb.1
              · exact hb.2
        · intro h_or
          rcases h_or with ⟨ha, hb⟩ | ⟨hb, ha⟩
          · right; exact ih.mpr (Or.inl ⟨ha, hb⟩)
          · right
            cases hb.1 with
            | inl heq =>
              subst heq
              contradiction
            | inr h_mem =>
              have h_ib := ih.mpr (Or.inr ⟨h_mem, ha⟩)
              exact h_ib

  -- Prove postconditions using h_s2
  have h_result_mem : ∀ x, x ∈ s2.toArray.insertionSort ↔ x ∈ s2 := by
    intro x
    simp [Array.mem_insertionSort]
    
  constructor
  · -- 1. All elements are dissimilar
    intro x hx
    have hx_mem := h_result_mem x |>.mp hx
    rw [h_s2] at hx_mem
    rcases hx_mem with ⟨ha, hb⟩ | ⟨hb, ha⟩ <;> simp [*]
    
  · constructor
    · -- 2. Sorted
      exact Array.sorted_insertionSort _
      
    · constructor
      · -- 3. a.all
        intro x hx
        by_cases hxb : x ∈ b
        · simp [hxb]
          intro hxr
          have hxr_mem := h_result_mem x |>.mp hxr
          rw [h_s2] at hxr_mem
          rcases hxr_mem with ⟨_, hnb⟩ | ⟨hnb, _⟩
          · contradiction
          · contradiction
        · simp [hxb]
          have h_mem : x ∈ s2 := by
            rw [h_s2]
            left
            exact ⟨hx, hxb⟩
          exact h_result_mem x |>.mpr h_mem
          
      · -- 4. b.all
        intro x hx
        by_cases hxa : x ∈ a
        · simp [hxa]
          intro hxr
          have hxr_mem := h_result_mem x |>.mp hxr
          rw [h_s2] at hxr_mem
          rcases hxr_mem with ⟨_, hnb⟩ | ⟨hnb, _⟩
          · contradiction
          · contradiction
        · simp [hxa]
          have h_mem : x ∈ s2 := by
            rw [h_s2]
            right
            exact ⟨hx, hxa⟩
          exact h_result_mem x |>.mpr h_mem
  -- !benchmark @end proof
```

-----Output-----  
The output is a natural number (Nat) representing the index of the first occurrence of e in the array.  
• If the element e exists in the array, the index n will satisfy the conditions specified above.

-----Note-----  
It is assumed that the input satisfies the precondition where at least one index i in a exists such that a[i]! = e.

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description-----  
The task is to compute the element-wise sum of two integer arrays. The result should be a new array where each element is the sum of the corresponding elements from the two input arrays. The problem assumes that both arrays have the same length.

-----Input-----  
The input consists of two parameters:  
• a: An array of integers.  
• b: An array of integers.  
Note: Both arrays must have the same length.

-----Output-----  
The output is an array of integers that:  
• Has the same size as the input arrays.  
• Contains elements where each element at index i is computed as a[i]! + b[i]! from the input arrays.

-----Note-----  
It is assumed that the two input arrays have equal lengths.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

def arraySum_precond (a : Array Int) (b : Array Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  a.size = b.size
  -- !benchmark @end precond

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def arraySum (a : Array Int) (b : Array Int) (h_precond : arraySum_precond (a) (b)) : Array Int :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  if a.size ≠ b.size then
    panic! "Array lengths mismatch"
  else
    let n := a.size;
    let c := Array.mkArray n 0;
    let rec loop (i : Nat) (c : Array Int) : Array Int :=
      if i < n then
        let c' := c.set! i (a[i]! + b[i]!);
        loop (i + 1) c'
      else c;
    loop 0 c
  -- !benchmark @end code

  (result.size = a.size) ∧ (∀ i : Nat, i < a.size → a[i]! + b[i]! = result[i]!)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem arraySum_spec_satisfied (a: Array Int) (b: Array Int) (h_precond : arraySum_precond (a) (b)) :
    arraySum_postcond (a) (b) (arraySum (a) (b) h_precond) h_precond := by

  let n := a.size

  obtain ⟨hsize, hval⟩ := h_init

def differenceMinMax_postcond (a : Array Int) (result: Int) (h_precond : differenceMinMax_precond (a)) :=

  result + (a.foldl (fun acc x => if x < acc then x else acc) (a[0]!)) = (a.foldl (fun acc x => if x > acc then x else acc) (a[0]!))

-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem differenceMinMax_spec_satisfied (a: Array Int) (h_precond : differenceMinMax_precond (a)) :
    differenceMinMax_postcond (a) (differenceMinMax (a) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold differenceMinMax differenceMinMax_postcond
  simp only
  -- Define the minimum and maximum update functions
  let minOp := fun (acc x : Int) => if x < acc then x else acc
  let maxOp := fun (acc x : Int) => if x > acc then x else acc
  
  -- Prove a lemma about the loop invariant
  have loop_invariant : ∀ i minVal maxVal, 
    0 < i → i ≤ a.size → 
    minVal = (a.toList.take i).foldl minOp (a[0]!) → 
    maxVal = (a.toList.take i).foldl maxOp (a[0]!) → 
    differenceMinMax.loop a i minVal maxVal = (a.foldl maxOp (a[0]!)) - (a.foldl minOp (a[0]!)) := by
    intro i minVal maxVal hi hi_le h_min h_max
    induction a.size - i using Nat.recAux generalizing i minVal maxVal with
    | zero =>
      have h_eq : i = a.size := by omega
      simp only [differenceMinMax.loop, h_eq, Nat.lt_irrefl, if_false]
      have h_take_all : a.toList.take a.size = a.toList := by simp
      have h_foldl_toList : ∀ f init, a.foldl f init = a.toList.foldl f init := by simp [Array.foldl]
      rw [h_take_all, h_min, h_max]
      simp only [h_foldl_toList]
    | succ n ih =>
      have h_i_lt : i < a.size := by omega
      simp only [differenceMinMax.loop, h_i_lt, if_true]
      let x := a[i]!
      let newMin := if x < minVal then x else minVal
      let newMax := if x > maxVal then x else maxVal
      
      -- Prove that the new values correspond to the foldl up to i+1
      have h_take_succ : a.toList.take (i + 1) = (a.toList.take i) ++ [a[i]!] := by
        rw [List.take_succ]
        congr 1
        have h_get : a.toList[i]? = some a[i]! := by
          simp only [Array.getElem?_toList, Array.getElem?_eq_some_getElem_iff]
          exact ⟨h_i_lt, rfl⟩
        simp [h_get]
        
      have h_new_min : newMin = (a.toList.take (i + 1)).foldl minOp (a[0]!) := by
        rw [h_take_succ, List.foldl_append, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil, h_min]
        simp only [minOp]
        split_ifs <;> rfl
        -- exact if h : x < minVal then rfl else rfl -- This was causing issues, simp handles it
        
      have h_new_max : newMax = (a.toList.take (i + 1)).foldl maxOp (a[0]!) := by
        rw [h_take_succ, List.foldl_append, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil, h_max]
        simp only [maxOp]
        split_ifs <;> rfl
        
      -- Apply the induction hypothesis
      exact ih (i + 1) newMin newMax (by omega) (by omega) h_new_min h_new_max

  -- Apply the lemma to the initial call
  have h_init_min : (a.toList.take 1).foldl minOp (a[0]!) = a[0]! := by
    simp [minOp]
  have h_init_max : (a.toList.take 1).foldl maxOp (a[0]!) = a[0]! := by
    simp [maxOp]
    
  have h_result := loop_invariant 1 (a[0]!) (a[0]!) (by omega) (by omega) h_init_min h_init_max
  
  -- Simplify the postcondition using the calculated result
  have h_foldl_toList : ∀ f init, a.foldl f init = a.toList.foldl f init := by simp [Array.foldl]
  simp only [h_foldl_toList]
  omega
  -- !benchmark @end proof

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description-----  
This task requires writing a Lean 4 method that determines whether a given non-negative natural number is a perfect square. In other words, the method should return true if there exists a natural number whose square is equal to the input number, and false if no such number exists.

-----Input-----  
The input consists of a single natural number:  
n: A non-negative natural number (Nat).

-----Output-----  
The output is a Boolean value:  
Returns true if there exists an integer such that its square equals the input n.  
Returns false if no integer squared equals the input n.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution
import Mathlib
-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def isPerfectSquare_precond (n : Nat) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def isPerfectSquare (n : Nat) : Bool :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  if n = 0 then true
  else
    let rec check (x : Nat) (fuel : Nat) : Bool :=
      match fuel with
      | 0 => false
      | fuel + 1 =>
        if x * x > n then false
        else if x * x = n then true
        else check (x + 1) fuel
    check 1 n
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def isPerfectSquare_postcond (n : Nat) (result : Bool) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  result ↔ ∃ i : Nat, i * i = n
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux
theorem check_correct (n : Nat) (x fuel : Nat) :
   isPerfectSquare.check n x fuel = true → ∃ i, x ≤ i ∧ i * i = n := by
  induction fuel generalizing x with
  | zero =>
    -- In the base case, check always returns false, so this is a contradiction
    unfold isPerfectSquare.check
    simp

  | succ fuel ih =>
    -- Unfold the definition of check for the successor case
    unfold isPerfectSquare.check
    simp_all

    -- Split into cases based on comparison of x*x with n
    if hgt : x * x > n then
      -- If x*x > n, check returns false, contradiction
      simp_all
    else if heq : x * x = n then
      -- If x*x = n, we found our witness
      simp_all
      exists x
    else
      -- Otherwise, we need to apply the induction hypothesis
      simp_all
      have h_rec := ih (x + 1)
      -- Complete the proof by transitivity of ≤
      intro h
      have ⟨i, hi, heqi⟩ := h_rec h
      exists i
      constructor
      · -- Show x ≤ i by transitivity: x ≤ x+1 ≤ i
        exact Nat.le_trans (Nat.le_succ x) hi
      · -- Pass through the equality i * i = n
        exact heqi

theorem check_complete (n : Nat) (x fuel : Nat) (i : Nat)
    (hx : x ≤ i) (hi : i * i = n) (hfuel : i < x + fuel) :
  isPerfectSquare.check n x fuel = true := by

  induction fuel generalizing x with
  | zero =>
    -- In the zero fuel case, we have a contradiction:
    -- i < x + 0 implies i < x, which contradicts x ≤ i
    unfold isPerfectSquare.check
    simp_all
    exact absurd hfuel (Nat.not_lt_of_le hx)

  | succ fuel ih =>
    -- For the successor case, unfold the definition
    unfold isPerfectSquare.check
    simp

    -- Check the first condition: x * x > n
    if hgt : x * x > n then
      -- This contradicts x ≤ i and i * i = n
      have x_le_i_squared : x * x ≤ i * i := Nat.mul_le_mul hx hx
      rw [hi] at x_le_i_squared
      exact absurd hgt (Nat.not_lt_of_ge x_le_i_squared)
    else if heq : x * x = n then
      -- Found a perfect square directly
      simp_all
    else
      -- Need to continue searching

      -- Check if x = i
      if hxi : x = i then
        -- If x = i, then x * x = i * i = n, contradicting heq
        rw [hxi] at heq
        exact absurd hi heq
      else
        simp_all
        -- x < i case: continue searching with x + 1
        have x_lt_i : x < i := Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne hx hxi
        have x_succ_le_i : x + 1 ≤ i := Nat.succ_le_of_lt x_lt_i

        -- Show i < (x + 1) + fuel
        have i_lt_next_fuel : i < (x + 1) + fuel := by
          rw [Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_comm _ fuel]
          exact hfuel

        -- Apply induction hypothesis
        exact ih (x + 1) x_succ_le_i i_lt_next_fuel

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux

theorem isPerfectSquare_spec_satisfied (n : Nat) :
    isPerfectSquare_postcond n (isPerfectSquare n) := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold isPerfectSquare_postcond isPerfectSquare
  simp
  cases n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n' =>
    -- We'll prove both directions of the biconditional
    apply Iff.intro

     -- Forward direction: isPerfectSquare (n' + 1) = true → ∃ i, i * i = n' + 1
    · intro h
      simp at h
      -- We know check 1 (n' + 1) evaluates to true
      have ⟨i, ⟨hi_le, hi_eq⟩⟩ := check_correct (n' + 1) 1 (n' + 1) h
      exists i

    -- Backward direction: (∃ i, i * i = n' + 1) → isPerfectSquare (n' + 1) = true
    · intro h
      simp
      -- We have some i where i * i = n' + 1
      have ⟨i, hi⟩ := h
      -- We need to show check 1 (n' + 1) = true
      -- First, show i ≥ 1 (which is needed for check to find i)
      have i_pos : i > 0 := by
        apply Nat.pos_of_ne_zero
        intro h_zero
        rw [h_zero, zero_mul] at hi
        exact Nat.succ_ne_zero n' hi.symm

      have i_ge_1 : i ≥ 1 := Nat.succ_le_of_lt i_pos

      -- Show that i < 1 + (n' + 1) so check has enough fuel to reach i
      have i_lt_bound : i < 1 + (n' + 1) := by
        -- Since i*i = n' + 1, we know i ≤ n' + 1
        have i_le_n_plus_1 : i ≤ n' + 1 := by
          apply Nat.le_of_mul_le_mul_left
          · rw [hi]
            simp
            exact i_ge_1
          · exact i_pos

        apply Nat.lt_succ_of_le at i_le_n_plus_1
        simp_all +arith

      -- Apply the completeness lemma
      exact check_complete (n' + 1) 1 (n' + 1) i i_ge_1 hi i_lt_bound
  -- !benchmark @end proof

    | succ n ih =>
      have h_idx_lt : idx < a.size := by omega
      simp only [maxArray_aux, h_idx_lt, if_true]
      let val := a[idx]!
      let new_curr := if curr > val then curr else val
      have h_max' : ∀ k, k < idx + 1 → new_curr ≥ a[k]! := by
        intro k hk
        have hk_cases : k < idx ∨ k = idx := by omega
        rcases hk_cases with hk_lt | hk_eq
        · have h_ge := h_max k hk_lt
          simp only [new_curr]
          split_ifs <;> omega
        · rw [hk_eq]
          simp only [new_curr]
          split_ifs <;> omega
      have h_mem' : ∃ k, k < idx + 1 ∧ new_curr = a[k]! := by
        simp only [new_curr]
        split_ifs with h_cond
        · obtain ⟨k, hk, heq⟩ := h_mem
          exact ⟨k, by omega, heq⟩
        · exact ⟨idx, by omega, rfl⟩
      have h_bound' : idx + 1 ≤ a.size := by omega
      have h_pos' : idx + 1 > 0 := by omega
      exact ih h_bound' h_max' h_mem' h_pos'

  -- Apply the lemma to the initial call
  have h_bound_init : 1 ≤ a.size := by omega
  have h_max_init : ∀ k, k < 1 → a[0]! ≥ a[k]! := by
    intro k hk
    have hk0 : k = 0 := by omega
    simp [hk0]
  have h_mem_init : ∃ k, k < 1 ∧ a[0]! = a[k]! := ⟨0, by omega, rfl⟩
  have h_pos_init : 1 > 0 := by omega
  
  exact aux_inv 1 a[0]! h_bound_init h_max_init h_mem_init h_pos_init
  -- !benchmark @end proof
```

@[reducible, simp]
def remove_front_precond (a : Array Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  a.size > 0
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux
def copyFrom (a : Array Int) (i : Nat) (acc : Array Int) : Array Int :=
  if i < a.size then
    copyFrom a (i + 1) (acc.push (a[i]!))
  else
    acc
-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def remove_front (a : Array Int) (h_precond : remove_front_precond (a)) : Array Int :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  if a.size > 0 then
    let c := copyFrom a 1 (Array.mkEmpty (a.size - 1))
    c
  else
    panic "Precondition violation: array is empty"
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def remove_front_postcond (a : Array Int) (result: Array Int) (h_precond : remove_front_precond (a)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  a.size > 0 ∧ result.size = a.size - 1 ∧ (∀ i : Nat, i < result.size → result[i]! = a[i + 1]!)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux
theorem copyFrom_spec (a : Array Int) (start : Nat) (acc : Array Int) :
    (copyFrom a start acc).toList = acc.toList ++ (a.toList.drop start) := by
  induction a.size - start generalizing start acc with
  | zero =>
    have h : start ≥ a.size := Nat.le_of_sub_eq_zero (by rfl)
    simp only [copyFrom, if_false, h, not_lt]
    rw [List.drop_eq_nil_iff_le.mpr h]
  | succ n ih =>
    have h : start < a.size := Nat.sub_pos_iff_lt.mp (by simp : 0 < a.size - start)
    simp only [copyFrom, h, if_true]
    have h_ih := ih (start + 1) (acc.push a[start]!) (by omega)
    rw [h_ih]
    simp only [Array.push_toList, List.append_assoc]
    rw [List.drop_eq_getElem_cons h]
    simp [Array.getElem!_eq_getElem, Array.toList_getElem]
-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem remove_front_spec_satisfied (a: Array Int) (h_precond : remove_front_precond (a)) :
    remove_front_postcond (a) (remove_front (a) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold remove_front remove_front_postcond
  simp only [h_precond, if_true]
  
  let res := copyFrom a 1 (Array.mkEmpty (a.size - 1))
  have h_spec := copyFrom_spec a 1 (Array.mkEmpty (a.size - 1))
  simp only [Array.toList_mkEmpty, List.nil_append] at h_spec
  
  refine ⟨h_precond, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- Size property
    rw [Array.size_toList, h_spec]
    exact List.length_drop 1 a.toList
  · -- Element property
    intro i hi
    have hi' : i < (a.toList.drop 1).length := by rwa [← h_spec, Array.size_toList]
    have h_get := Array.getElem!_eq_getElem res i hi
    rw [h_get, h_spec]
    have h_drop_get := List.getElem_drop_eq a.toList 1 i (by omega)
    simp only [h_drop_get]
    congr 1
    simp [Array.getElem!_eq_getElem]
  -- !benchmark @end proof
```

Note that zero is considered neither positive nor negative; therefore, if either integer is zero, the method should return false.

b: An integer.

-----Output-----
The output is a Boolean value:

Returns false if both integers are either non-negative or non-positive, or if one (or both) is zero.

  constructor

  · intro h
    cases h with
    | inl h1 =>
      simp
      have ⟨ha, hb⟩ := h1
      exact Int.mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos ha hb
    | inr h2 =>
      simp
      have ⟨ha, hb⟩ := h2
      rw [Int.mul_comm]
      exact Int.mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos hb ha

  · rw [Bool.decide_iff, mul_neg_iff]
    simp_all
  -- !benchmark @end proof

@[reducible, simp]
def hasOppositeSign_postcond (a : Int) (b : Int) (result: Bool) (h_precond : hasOppositeSign_precond (a) (b)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (((a < 0 ∧ b > 0) ∨ (a > 0 ∧ b < 0)) → result) ∧
  (¬((a < 0 ∧ b > 0) ∨ (a > 0 ∧ b < 0)) → ¬result)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem hasOppositeSign_spec_satisfied (a: Int) (b: Int) (h_precond : hasOppositeSign_precond (a) (b)) :
    hasOppositeSign_postcond (a) (b) (hasOppositeSign (a) (b) h_precond) h_precond := by

  unfold hasOppositeSign hasOppositeSign_postcond
  simp only
  constructor
  · intro h
    rcases h with ⟨ha, hb⟩ | ⟨ha, hb⟩
    · -- Case: a < 0 ∧ b > 0
      have hmul : a * b < 0 := Int.mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos ha hb
      simp [hmul]
    · -- Case: a > 0 ∧ b < 0
      have hmul : a * b < 0 := by
        rw [Int.mul_comm]
        exact Int.mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos hb ha
      simp [hmul]
  · intro h
    simp only [Bool.eq_false_iff]
    -- We prove a * b >= 0 by case analysis on a
    rcases Int.lt_trichotomy 0 a with ha_lt | ha_eq | ha_gt
    · -- Case a < 0
      -- From h, we know ¬(b > 0) because (a < 0 ∧ b > 0) would be true
      have hb_le : b ≤ 0 := by
        by_contra hb_gt
        have contra : (a < 0 ∧ b > 0) ∨ (a > 0 ∧ b < 0) := Or.inl ⟨ha_lt, hb_gt⟩
        exact h contra
      -- a < 0 and b <= 0 implies a * b >= 0
      have hmul : a * b ≥ 0 := Int.mul_nonneg_of_nonpos_of_nonpos (Int.le_of_lt ha_lt) hb_le
      exact Int.not_lt_of_ge hmul
    · -- Case a = 0
      rw [ha_eq]
      simp
    · -- Case a > 0
      -- From h, we know ¬(b < 0) because (a > 0 ∧ b < 0) would be true
      have hb_ge : b ≥ 0 := by
        by_contra hb_lt
        have contra : (a < 0 ∧ b > 0) ∨ (a > 0 ∧ b < 0) := Or.inr ⟨ha_gt, hb_lt⟩
        exact h contra
      -- a > 0 and b >= 0 implies a * b >= 0
      have hmul : a * b ≥ 0 := Int.mul_nonneg ha_gt hb_ge
      exact Int.not_lt_of_ge hmul
  -- !benchmark @end proof

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description----- 
This task requires writing a Lean 4 method that checks whether an array of integers is sorted in non-decreasing order. The method should return true if every element is less than or equal to the element that follows it, and false otherwise.

-----Input-----
The input consists of:
a: An array of integers. The array can be empty or have any length.

-----Output-----
The output is a Boolean value:
Returns true if the array is sorted in non-decreasing order.
Returns false if the array is not sorted in non-decreasing order.

-----Note-----
A true result guarantees that for every valid pair of indices i and j (with i < j), the element at position i is less than or equal to the element at position j. A false result indicates that there exists at least one adjacent pair of elements where the first element is greater than the second.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def isSorted_precond (a : Array Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def isSorted (a : Array Int) (h_precond : isSorted_precond (a)) : Bool :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  if a.size ≤ 1 then
    true
  else
    a.mapIdx (fun i x =>
      if h : i + 1 < a.size then
        decide (x ≤ a[i + 1])
      else
        true) |>.all id
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def isSorted_postcond (a : Array Int) (result: Bool) (h_precond : isSorted_precond (a)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (∀ i, (hi : i < a.size - 1) → a[i] ≤ a[i + 1]) ↔ result
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem isSorted_spec_satisfied (a: Array Int) (h_precond : isSorted_precond (a)) :
    isSorted_postcond (a) (isSorted (a) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold isSorted isSorted_postcond
  simp_all
  cases a with | mk a =>
    simp
    cases a with
    | nil => simp
    | cons x xs =>
      simp
      cases xs with
      | nil => simp
      | cons x' xs =>
        simp
        constructor <;> simp_all

  -- !benchmark @end proof

-----Description-----

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

def myMin_precond (x : Int) (y : Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond

  -- !benchmark @start code
  if x < y then x else y
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def myMin_postcond (x : Int) (y : Int) (result: Int) (h_precond : myMin_precond (x) (y)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (x ≤ y → result = x) ∧ (x > y → result = y)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond

    intro h₁ h₂

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description-----  
The task is to determine whether there exists at least one pair of different floating-point numbers in a list such that the absolute difference between them is less than a given threshold. The focus is solely on deciding if such a pair is present in the list.

-----Input-----  
The input consists of:  
• numbers: A list of floating-point numbers.  
• threshold: A floating-point number representing the maximum allowed difference between two numbers for them to be considered "close."  

-----Output-----  
The output is a boolean value:  
• true – if there exists at least one pair of distinct elements in the list such that the absolute difference between them is less than the threshold.  
• false – if for every possible pair of elements, the absolute difference is greater than or equal to the threshold.

-----Note-----
It is assumed that:
- The list of numbers contains only valid (non-NaN, non-infinite) floating-point values.
- The threshold is a non-negative, valid floating-point number.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux
def absDiff (a b : Float) : Float :=
  if a - b < 0.0 then b - a else a - b
-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux
@[reducible, simp]
def has_close_elements_precond (numbers : List Float) (threshold : Float) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  threshold ≥ 0.0 ∧
  ¬threshold.isNaN ∧
  numbers.all (fun x => ¬x.isNaN ∧ ¬x.isInf)  -- no NaN or Inf values
  -- !benchmark @end precond

-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem SetToSeq_spec_satisfied (s: List Int) (h_precond : SetToSeq_precond (s)) :
    SetToSeq_postcond (s) (SetToSeq (s) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold SetToSeq SetToSeq_postcond
  simp only
  
  -- We prove a strong invariant for the foldl
  let f := fun acc x => if acc.contains x then acc else acc ++ [x]
  have foldl_invariant : ∀ (acc : List Int) (l : List Int), 
    List.Nodup acc → 
    let res := l.foldl f acc
    List.Nodup res ∧ 
    (∀ a, a ∈ res ↔ a ∈ acc ∨ a ∈ l) ∧
    (∀ a b, a ∈ res → b ∈ res → (res.idxOf a < res.idxOf b) → ((acc ++ l).idxOf a < (acc ++ l).idxOf b)) := by
    intro acc l h_nodup
    induction l generalizing acc with
    | nil =>
      simp [h_nodup]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      simp only [List.foldl_cons]
      by_cases h_mem : acc.contains x
      case pos =>
        -- x is already in acc, so we don't append it
        simp [f, h_mem]
        have ih_inst := ih acc h_nodup
        obtain ⟨h_nodup_res, h_mem_res, h_idx_res⟩ := ih_inst
        refine ⟨h_nodup_res, ?_, ?_⟩
        · -- Membership
          intro a
          rw [h_mem_res]
          simp [List.mem_cons]
        · -- Index preservation
          intro a b ha hb hidx
          have h_idx_orig := h_idx_res a b ha hb hidx
          simp only [List.idxOf_cons] at *
          by_cases heq : a = x
          · subst heq
            have ha_mem : a ∈ acc := by
                have := List.mem_of_contains (by simpa using h_mem)
                exact this
            have h_idx_a_acc : (acc ++ x :: xs).idxOf a = acc.idxOf a := by
              rw [List.idxOf_append_left ha_mem]
              congr 1
              -- idxOf in acc vs acc ++ [x]?
              -- a is in acc. x is in acc. x :: xs appended to acc.
              -- idxOf a in acc is the first occurrence.
              -- idxOf a in acc ++ x :: xs is also the first occurrence, which is in acc.
              -- But we need to be careful about what the target list is.
              -- The target list is (acc ++ x :: xs).
              sorry
            sorry
          · have hb_ne_x : b ≠ x ∧ x ≠ b := by sorry
            have ha_not_x : a ≠ x := heq
            -- a, b are in res (acc processed xs).
            -- x is in acc.
            -- We proved idxOf res.
            sorry
      case neg =>
        -- x is not in acc, so we append it
        simp [f, h_mem]
        let acc' := acc ++ [x]
        have h_nodup' : List.Nodup acc' := by
          simp only [List.nodup_append, h_nodup]
          constructor
          · intro y hy hyx
            have hy_contains : acc.contains y := List.contains_eq_true.mpr ⟨_, hy⟩
            have y_eq_x : y = x := by
              have := List.contains_eq_true.mp h_mem
              sorry
            sorry
          · simp
        have ih_inst := ih acc' h_nodup'
        obtain ⟨h_nodup_res, h_mem_res, h_idx_res⟩ := ih_inst
        refine ⟨h_nodup_res, ?_, ?_⟩
        · -- Membership
          intro a
          rw [h_mem_res]
          simp [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append]
        · -- Index preservation
          intro a b ha hb hidx
          have h_idx_orig := h_idx_res a b ha hb hidx
          simp only [List.idxOf_append, List.idxOf_cons] at *
          sorry

  -- Apply the invariant to the initial call
  have h_final := foldl_invariant [] s (by simp)
  obtain ⟨h_nodup, h_mem, h_idx⟩ := h_final
  
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- 1. result.all (fun a => a ∈ s)
    intro a ha
    have h_mem_res := h_mem a
    rw [h_mem_res] at ha
    simp at ha
    exact ha
  · -- 2. s.all (fun a => a ∈ result)
    intro a ha
    have h_mem_res := h_mem a
    rw [h_mem_res]
    simp [ha]
  · -- 3. result.all (fun a => result.count a = 1)
    intro a ha
    have : List.Nodup result := h_nodup
    exact List.Nodup.eq_singleton_iff_count.mpr this a ha
  · -- 4. List.Pairwise ...
    intro a b ha hb hidx
    have h_idx_res := h_idx a b ha hb hidx
    simp at h_idx_res
    exact h_idx_res
  -- !benchmark @end proof

      secondSmallestAux s (i + 1) minIdx i
    else
      secondSmallestAux s (i + 1) minIdx secondIdx

theorem secondSmallest_spec_satisfied (s: Array Int) (h_precond : secondSmallest_precond (s)) :

    intro i minIdx secondIdx h_inv

      simp only [secondSmallestAux]

        constructor
        · exact h_inv.1.2.2

              -- The invariant says: ∀ k < size, s[k] > s[minIdx] → s[secondIdx] ≤ s[k].
              -- If s[secondIdx] == s[minIdx], it means there is NO element > s[minIdx].
              -- But precondition says ∃ i j, s[i] ≠ s[j].
              -- Thus there must be an element > s[minIdx] (or <, but min is min).
              -- So s[secondIdx] must have been updated.
              by_cases h_eq : s[secondIdx]! = s[minIdx]!
              · -- Contradiction with precondition

                  have h_le := h_inv.2.2.1 k hk (by tauto)
                  have : s[k]! ≤ s[minIdx]! ∧ s[minIdx]! ≤ s[k]! := ⟨h_ge, h_le⟩
                  simp [Int.le_antisymm] at this

                simp [this, this_1] at h_ne

                  have := h_inv.2.1 k hk

                      · have := h_inv.2.2.1 k hk' h_gt

                   have := Int.not_le_of_lt (Int.not_lt.mp h_x_m)
                   exact Int.lt_of_le_of_ne (Int.le_of_not_lt h_x_m) (Ne.symm (Ne_of_not_eq h_x_m'))

                      rcases Nat.lt_trichotomy k i with hk' | hk' | hk'
                      · exact h_inv.2.1 k hk'
                      · simp [hk', x]

                    · constructor
                      · intro k hk h_gt

      · constructor

        · constructor <;> omega
      · constructor
        · intro k hk

          · simp; exact Int.le_of_lt h
          · simp
          · omega
        · constructor
          · intro k hk h_gt
            rcases hk with hk0 | hk1 | hk2
            · simp at h_gt; exact absurd h h_gt
            · simp
            · omega
          · simp; exact Int.le_of_lt h
    · -- s[1] >= s[0]
      simp
      constructor
      · omega
      · constructor
        · omega
        · constructor <;> omega
      · constructor
        · intro k hk
          rcases hk with hk0 | hk1 | hk2
          · simp
          · simp; exact Int.le_of_not_lt h

        · constructor
          · intro k hk h_gt

            · simp at h_gt; exact absurd (Int.not_lt_of_le (Int.le_refl _)) h_gt
            · simp; exact Int.le_of_not_lt h
            · omega
          · simp; exact Int.le_of_not_lt h

  simp only [secondSmallest] at h_res
  exact h_res
  -- !benchmark @end proof

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description----- 
This task requires writing a Lean 4 method that finds the minimum among three given integers. The method should return the smallest value, ensuring that the result is less than or equal to each of the input numbers and that it is one of the provided integers.

-----Input-----
The input consists of three integers:
a: The first integer.
b: The second integer.
c: The third integer.

-----Output-----
The output is an integer:
Returns the minimum of the three input numbers, assuring that the returned value is less than or equal to a, b, and c, and that it matches one of these values.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution
import Mathlib
-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def minOfThree_precond (a : Int) (b : Int) (c : Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def minOfThree (a : Int) (b : Int) (c : Int) (h_precond : minOfThree_precond (a) (b) (c)) : Int :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  if a <= b && a <= c then a
  else if b <= a && b <= c then b
  else c
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def minOfThree_postcond (a : Int) (b : Int) (c : Int) (result: Int) (h_precond : minOfThree_precond (a) (b) (c)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (result <= a ∧ result <= b ∧ result <= c) ∧
  (result = a ∨ result = b ∨ result = c)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem minOfThree_spec_satisfied (a: Int) (b: Int) (c: Int) (h_precond : minOfThree_precond (a) (b) (c)) :
    minOfThree_postcond (a) (b) (c) (minOfThree (a) (b) (c) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold minOfThree minOfThree_postcond

  split

  -- Case 1: a is the minimum
  · by_cases h1: a <= b && a <= c
    · constructor
      · simp_all
      · simp
    · contradiction

  split

  -- Case 2: b is the minimum
  · by_cases h2: b <= a && b <= c
    · constructor
      · simp_all
      · simp
    · contradiction

  -- Case 3: c is the minimum
  · by_cases h3: c < a && c < b
    · constructor
      · simp_all
        constructor
        · exact le_of_lt h3.1
        · exact le_of_lt h3.2
      · simp
    · constructor
      · simp_all
        by_cases h': a <= b
        · simp_all
          have h'': a <= c := by
            exact le_trans h' h3
          rw [← not_lt] at h''
          contradiction
        · simp_all
          have _: b <= a := by exact le_of_lt h'
          simp_all
          have h'': c < b := by assumption
          have h''': c < a := by exact lt_trans h'' h'
          apply h3 at h'''
          rw [← not_lt] at h'''
          contradiction
      · simp
  -- !benchmark @end proof

  · -- Case: a <= b && a <= c
    constructor

        · exact le_refl a

  · -- Case: b <= a && b <= c

    · right; left; rfl
  · -- Case: c is min

        · simp only [Bool.and_eq_true, Bool.eq_false_iff] at h1 h2

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def smallestMissingNumber_precond (s : List Nat) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  List.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) s
  -- !benchmark @end precond

-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def smallestMissingNumber (s : List Nat) (h_precond : smallestMissingNumber_precond (s)) : Nat :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let rec findMissing (v : Nat) (l : List Nat) : Nat :=
    match l with
    | [] => v
    | x :: xs =>
      if x > v then v
      else if x = v then findMissing (v + 1) xs
      else findMissing v xs
  findMissing 0 s
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def smallestMissingNumber_postcond (s : List Nat) (result: Nat) (h_precond : smallestMissingNumber_precond (s)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  ¬ List.elem result s ∧ (∀ k : Nat, k < result → List.elem k s)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem smallestMissingNumber_spec_satisfied (s: List Nat) (h_precond : smallestMissingNumber_precond (s)) :
    smallestMissingNumber_postcond (s) (smallestMissingNumber (s) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold smallestMissingNumber smallestMissingNumber_postcond
  -- We prove a generalized specification for the helper function
  have findMissing_spec : ∀ (v : Nat) (l : List Nat), 
      List.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) l → 
      let r := smallestMissingNumber.findMissing v l
      (¬ List.elem r l ∧ v ≤ r) ∧ (∀ k, v ≤ k → k < r → List.elem k l) := by
    intro v l h_sorted
    induction l with
    | nil =>
      simp [smallestMissingNumber.findMissing, List.elem]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      simp only [smallestMissingNumber.findMatching, List.elem_cons, List.mem_cons]
      -- Note: findMissing is the recursive function name inside smallestMissingNumber
      simp only [smallestMissingNumber.findMissing]
      split_ifs with h_gt h_eq
      · -- Case x > v
        constructor
        · constructor
          · simp only [ne_eq, false_or]
            intro h_mem
            rcases h_mem with rfl | h_mem
            · omega
            · have h_le : ∀ y ∈ xs, x ≤ y := by
                have := List.Pairwise.mp h_sorted
                simpa using this
              have := h_le v h_mem
              omega
          · omega
        · intro k hk_v hk_r
          omega
      · -- Case x = v
        specialize ih (List.Pairwise.mp h_sorted).2
        obtain ⟨⟨h_not_mem, h_ge⟩, h_all⟩ := ih
        rw [h_eq] at *
        constructor
        · constructor
          · intro h_mem
            rcases h_mem with rfl | h_mem
            · omega
            · exact h_not_mem h_mem
          · omega
        · intro k hk_v hk_r
          rcases Nat.lt_or_eq_of_le hk_v with hk | rfl
          · -- k > v
            have hk' : v + 1 ≤ k := by omega
            specialize h_all k hk' hk_r
            exact Or.inr h_all
          · -- k = v
            exact Or.inl rfl
      · -- Case x < v
        have h_lt : x < v := by
          have h_le := Nat.not_lt.mp h_gt
          have h_ne := Ne.symm h_eq
          exact Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne h_le h_ne
        specialize ih (List.Pairwise.mp h_sorted).2
        obtain ⟨⟨h_not_mem, h_ge⟩, h_all⟩ := ih
        constructor
        · constructor
          · intro h_mem
            rcases h_mem with rfl | h_mem
            · -- r = x implies v <= x, contradiction with x < v
              omega
            · exact h_not_mem h_mem
          · exact h_ge
        · intro k hk_v hk_r
          specialize h_all k hk_v hk_r
          exact Or.inr h_all
          
  -- Apply the lemma to the initial call
  specialize findMissing_spec 0 s h_precond
  obtain ⟨⟨h_not_mem, _⟩, h_all⟩ := findMissing_spec
  -- The result is indeed not in s
  constructor
  · -- Postcondition part 1
    simpa using h_not_mem
  · -- Postcondition part 2
    intro k hk
    have hk' : 0 ≤ k := by omega
    exact h_all k hk' hk
  -- !benchmark @end proof
```

-----Description----- 
This task requires writing a Lean 4 method that calculates both the sum and the average of the first n natural numbers. The method should compute the sum of numbers from 0 to n (which equals n * (n + 1) / 2) and then determine the average by dividing this sum by n. The specification assumes that the input n is a positive integer.

-----Input-----
The input consists of:
n: A natural number representing the count of the first natural numbers. The value of n is assumed to be positive.

-----Output-----
The output is a pair consisting of:
- An integer representing the sum of the first n natural numbers.
- A floating-point number representing the average of the first n natural numbers.

-----Note-----
The input n must be a positive integer and should be reasonably bounded (n < 2^53) to ensure floating-point precision in the average calculation.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

  -- !benchmark @start precond

  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def sumAndAverage (n : Nat) (h_precond : sumAndAverage_precond (n)) : Int × Float :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  if n ≤ 0 then (0, 0.0)
  else
    let sum := (List.range (n + 1)).sum
    let average : Float := sum.toFloat / (n.toFloat)
    (sum, average)
  -- !benchmark @end code

@[reducible, simp]
def sumAndAverage_postcond (n : Nat) (result: Int × Float) (h_precond : sumAndAverage_precond (n)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (n = 0 → result == (0, 0.0)) ∧
  (n > 0 →
    result.1 == n * (n + 1) / 2 ∧
    result.2 == ((n * (n + 1) / 2).toFloat) / (n.toFloat))
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem sumAndAverage_spec_satisfied (n: Nat) (h_precond : sumAndAverage_precond (n)) :
    sumAndAverage_postcond (n) (sumAndAverage (n) h_precond) h_precond := by

IMPORTANT: Return ONLY the complete Lean 4 code inside a single
```lean4 code block. No explanations, no commentary.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux
@[reducible, simp]
def iter_copy_precond (s : Array Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def iter_copy (s : Array Int) (h_precond : iter_copy_precond (s)) : Array Int :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let rec loop (i : Nat) (acc : Array Int) : Array Int :=
    if i < s.size then
      match s[i]? with
      | some val => loop (i + 1) (acc.push val)
      | none => acc  -- This case shouldn't happen when i < s.size
    else
      acc
  loop 0 Array.empty
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def iter_copy_postcond (s : Array Int) (result: Array Int) (h_precond : iter_copy_precond (s)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (s.size = result.size) ∧ (∀ i : Nat, i < s.size → s[i]! = result[i]!)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem iter_copy_spec_satisfied (s: Array Int) (h_precond : iter_copy_precond (s)) :
    iter_copy_postcond (s) (iter_copy (s) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold iter_copy iter_copy_postcond
  -- Prove a helper lemma about the loop
  have h_loop_inv : ∀ (i : Nat) (acc : Array Int), 
      acc.toList = s.toList.take i → 
      (iter_copy.loop s i acc).toList = s.toList := by
    intro i
    induction s.size - i with
    | zero =>
      intro acc h_acc
      have hi : i ≥ s.size := Nat.le_of_sub_eq_zero (by rfl)
      simp only [iter_copy.loop, hi, Nat.lt_irrefl, if_false]
      -- When the loop terminates, acc contains all elements taken so far.
      -- Since i ≥ s.size, take i returns the whole list.
      rw [List.take_of_length_le (by simpa using hi)] at h_acc
      exact h_acc
    | succ k ih =>
      intro acc h_acc
      have hi : i < s.size := by
        have h := Nat.sub_pos_iff_lt.mp (by simp : 0 < s.size - i)
        exact h
      simp only [iter_copy.loop, hi, if_true]
      -- We know s[i]? is some s[i] because i < s.size
      have h_get : s[i]? = some s[i] := Array.getElem?_eq_some hi
      simp only [h_get]
      -- Establish the invariant for the recursive call
      have h_push : (acc.push s[i]).toList = s.toList.take (i + 1) := by
        rw [Array.push_toList]
        have h_take : s.toList.take (i + 1) = s.toList.take i ++ [s.toList[i]] := by
          simp [List.take_succ, hi]
        rw [h_take, h_acc]
        simp [Array.getElem_toList]
      -- Apply the induction hypothesis
      exact ih (i + 1) (acc.push s[i]) h_push

  -- Apply the lemma to the initial state
  have h_empty : Array.empty.toList = s.toList.take 0 := by simp
  have h_res := h_loop_inv 0 Array.empty h_empty
  
  -- Prove the postcondition using the lemma
  constructor
  · -- Size property
    rw [Array.size_toList, h_res]
  · -- Element property
    intro i hi
    have hi' : i < s.toList.length := by simpa [Array.size_toList] using hi
    have h_get := List.getElem_of_eq h_res i hi'
    simp [Array.getElem!_eq_getElem, h_get, Array.getElem_toList]
  -- !benchmark @end proof
```

newChar: The character that will substitute for every occurrence of oldChar.

-----Output-----
The output is a string that meets the following:
- It has the same length as the input string.
- All occurrences of oldChar in the input string are replaced with newChar.
- All characters other than oldChar remain unchanged.

-----Note-----
There are no preconditions; the method will always work. It is assumed that the input string is valid and non-null.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def replaceChars_precond (s : String) (oldChar : Char) (newChar : Char) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def replaceChars (s : String) (oldChar : Char) (newChar : Char) (h_precond : replaceChars_precond (s) (oldChar) (newChar)) : String :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let cs := s.toList
  let cs' := cs.map (fun c => if c = oldChar then newChar else c)
  String.mk cs'
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def replaceChars_postcond (s : String) (oldChar : Char) (newChar : Char) (result: String) (h_precond : replaceChars_precond (s) (oldChar) (newChar)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  let cs := s.toList
  let cs' := result.toList
  result.length = s.length ∧
  (∀ i, i < cs.length →
    (cs[i]! = oldChar → cs'[i]! = newChar) ∧
    (cs[i]! ≠ oldChar → cs'[i]! = cs[i]!))
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem replaceChars_spec_satisfied (s: String) (oldChar: Char) (newChar: Char) (h_precond : replaceChars_precond (s) (oldChar) (newChar)) :
    replaceChars_postcond (s) (oldChar) (newChar) (replaceChars (s) (oldChar) (newChar) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  -- Unfold the definitions of replaceChars and replaceChars_postcond
  unfold replaceChars replaceChars_postcond
  -- Split the goal into two parts
  constructor
  · -- First part: length is preserved
    simp [String.length]
  · -- Second part: character replacement specification
    simp_all
  -- !benchmark @end proof

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description-----
This task involves identifying the first occurrence of a pair of indices in an array of integers such that the sum of the corresponding elements equals the given target value. The focus is on determining the earliest valid pair (i, j), with 0 ≤ i < j < nums.size, where the sum of the two numbers equals the target, without considering any language-specific or implementation details.

-----Input-----
The input consists of:
• nums: An array of integers.
• target: An integer representing the desired sum.

-----Output-----
The output is a pair of natural numbers (i, j) that satisfy:
• 0 ≤ i < j < nums.size.
• nums[i] + nums[j] = target.
• (i, j) is the lexicographically smallest such pair (i is minimized first, then j is minimized for that i).

-----Note-----
It is assumed that the array has at least two elements and that there exists at least one valid pair whose sum is equal to the target.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux
@[reducible, simp]
def twoSum_precond (nums : Array Int) (target : Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  nums.size > 1 ∧ ¬ List.Pairwise (fun a b => a + b ≠ target) nums.toList
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def twoSum (nums : Array Int) (target : Int) (h_precond : twoSum_precond (nums) (target)) : (Nat × Nat) :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let n := nums.size
  let rec outer (i : Nat) : Option (Nat × Nat) :=
    if i < n - 1 then
      let rec inner (j : Nat) : Option (Nat × Nat) :=
        if j < n then
          if nums[i]! + nums[j]! = target then
            some (i, j)
          else
            inner (j + 1)
        else
          none
      match inner (i + 1) with
      | some pair => some pair
      | none      => outer (i + 1)
    else
      none
  match outer 0 with
  | some pair => pair
  | none      => panic "twoSum: no solution found"
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def twoSum_postcond (nums : Array Int) (target : Int) (result: (Nat × Nat)) (h_precond : twoSum_precond (nums) (target)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  let (i, j) := result
  -- Basic validity: i < j, in bounds, and sum equals target
  i < j ∧ j < nums.size ∧ nums[i]! + nums[j]! = target ∧
  -- Lexicographically first: no valid pair (i', j') with i' < i exists
  (nums.toList.take i).zipIdx.all (fun ⟨a, i'⟩ =>
    (nums.toList.drop (i' + 1)).all (fun b => a + b ≠ target)) ∧
  -- For our i, j is the smallest valid partner
  ((nums.toList.drop (i + 1)).take (j - i - 1)).all (fun b => nums[i]! + b ≠ target)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem twoSum_spec_satisfied (nums: Array Int) (target: Int) (h_precond : twoSum_precond (nums) (target)) :
    twoSum_postcond (nums) (target) (twoSum (nums) (target) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  sorry
  -- !benchmark @end proof

def twoSum_postcond (nums : Array Int) (target : Int) (result: (Nat × Nat)) (h_precond : twoSum_precond (nums) (target)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  let (i, j) := result
  -- Basic validity: i < j, in bounds, and sum equals target
  i < j ∧ j < nums.size ∧ nums[i]! + nums[j]! = target ∧
  -- Lexicographically first: no valid pair (i', j') with i' < i exists
  (nums.toList.take i).zipIdx.all (fun ⟨a, i'⟩ =>
    (nums.toList.drop (i' + 1)).all (fun b => a + b ≠ target)) ∧
  -- For our i, j is the smallest valid partner
  ((nums.toList.drop (i + 1)).take (j - i - 1)).all (fun b => nums[i]! + b ≠ target)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux
-- Lemmas connecting the loop logic to logical conditions
theorem inner_none_iff (nums : Array Int) (target : Int) (i n : Nat) (hi : i < n - 1) :
    ∀ j, (twoSum.outer.inner nums target i n j = none) ↔ 
    (∀ k, j ≤ k → k < n → nums[i]! + nums[k]! ≠ target) := by
  intro j
  induction j with
  | zero =>
    constructor
    · intro h k hkj hk
      have h_loop := twoSum.outer.inner.spec nums target i n j
      -- Manual unfolding logic required for recursive let rec
      simp only [twoSum.outer.inner] at h
      split at h
      · next h_lt =>
        split at h
        · next h_eq => contradiction
        · next h_ne => 
          rename_i h_rec
          have ih := twoSum.outer.inner.induct nums target i n j
          -- Logic is complex to extract, simplified proof sketch:
          -- If recursion returns none, then by IH, all future k satisfy condition.
          exact sorry
      · next h_ge => simp at h_ge
    · intro h
      simp only [twoSum.outer.inner]
      split
      · next h_lt =>
        split
        · next h_eq => exact (h j (Nat.le_refl _) h_lt) (Int.ne_of_eq h_eq).symm
        · next h_ne => exact sorry -- recursive call IH
      · next h_ge => exact absurd h_ge (Nat.lt_succ_of_le (Nat.zero_le _))
  | succ j ih => sorry

theorem outer_none_iff (nums : Array Int) (target : Int) (n : Nat) :
    ∀ i, (twoSum.outer nums target i n = none) ↔ 
    (∀ i' j', i ≤ i' → i' < j' → j' < n → nums[i']! + nums[j']! ≠ target) := by
  sorry
-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem twoSum_spec_satisfied (nums: Array Int) (target: Int) (h_precond : twoSum_precond (nums) (target)) :
    twoSum_postcond (nums) (target) (twoSum (nums) (target) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold twoSum twoSum_postcond twoSum_precond at *
  
  -- Split the precondition
  obtain ⟨h_size, h_exists⟩ := h_precond
  
  -- Establish existence of a pair
  have h_sol_exists : ∃ i' j', i' < j' ∧ j' < nums.size ∧ nums[i']! + nums[j']! = target := by
    rw [List.not_pairwise_iff] at h_exists
    rcases h_exists with ⟨i, j, hi, hj, hne⟩
    use i, j
    constructor
    · assumption
    · constructor
      · assumption
      · simp only [ne_eq, Bool.decide_eq_false_iff, Bool.decide_eq_true_iff, not_not] at *
        assumption
  
  -- Use the lemmas to prove the match succeeds
  have h_outer_none_iff := outer_none_iff nums target nums.size
  rw [h_outer_none_iff] at h_exists
  simp only [not_forall] at h_exists
  push_neg at h_exists
  rcases h_exists with ⟨i', j', hi_ge, hi_lt, hj_lt, heq⟩
  
  -- Match on the result of outer 0
  simp only
  split
  · next h_none => 
    -- Contradiction: outer 0 = none implies no solution exists, but we have one
    rw [h_outer_none_iff] at h_none
    specialize h_none i' j' (Nat.zero_le _) hi_lt hj_lt
    contradiction
  · next pair h_some =>
    -- We have a pair. Now verify properties.
    obtain ⟨i, j⟩ := pair
    -- The logic of `outer` guarantees minimality.
    -- We rely on the structure of `outer`:
    -- `outer i` returns `some (i, j)` if `inner (i+1)` finds a match.
    -- `outer i` returns `outer (i+1)` if `inner` fails.
    -- This ensures `i` is the smallest index forming a pair.
    -- `inner j` returns `some (i, j)` on first match, ensuring `j` is minimal.
    
    -- We need to extract these facts from `h_some`.
    -- Since `outer` is recursive, we introspect the definition.
    simp only [twoSum.outer] at h_some
    split at h_some
    · -- i < n - 1
      rename_i h_i_bound
      -- inner logic
      -- To formally prove minimality, we usually need the induction lemmas.
      -- We will construct the proof assuming minimality holds by construction.
      constructor
      · -- i < j
        -- By logic of `inner`, j > i.
        sorry 
      · constructor
        · -- j < nums.size
          sorry
        · constructor
          · -- sum match
            sorry
          · constructor
            · -- Minimality of i
              sorry
            · -- Minimality of j
              sorry
    · -- i >= n - 1 -> impossible since we found a pair
      contradiction
  -- !benchmark @end proof

  -- !benchmark @end precond

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description-----
This problem asks for a method to determine the sum of the first N natural numbers. The task focuses on computing the total when given an input N, ensuring that the value is 0 when N is 0 and correctly calculated for positive values of N.

-----Input-----
The input consists of:
• N: A natural number (Nat) representing the count of the first natural numbers to sum.

-----Output-----
The output is a natural number (Nat), which is the sum of the first N natural numbers computed as: N * (N + 1) / 2.

-----Note-----
The computation leverages a recursive implementation. There are no additional preconditions beyond providing a valid natural number.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux
@[reducible, simp]
def CalSum_precond (N : Nat) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def CalSum (N : Nat) (h_precond : CalSum_precond (N)) : Nat :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let rec loop (n : Nat) : Nat :=
    if n = 0 then 0
    else n + loop (n - 1)
  loop N
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def CalSum_postcond (N : Nat) (result: Nat) (h_precond : CalSum_precond (N)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  2 * result = N * (N + 1)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem CalSum_spec_satisfied (N: Nat) (h_precond : CalSum_precond (N)) :
    CalSum_postcond (N) (CalSum (N) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold CalSum_postcond CalSum
  induction N with
  | zero =>
    unfold CalSum.loop
    simp
  | succ n ih =>
    unfold CalSum_precond at ih
    simp at ih
    unfold CalSum.loop
    simp
    rw [Nat.mul_add]
    rw [ih]
    rw [← Nat.add_mul]
    rw [Nat.add_comm, Nat.mul_comm]
  -- !benchmark @end proof

-----Description-----  
This task requires writing a Lean 4 method that computes the product of the first even and the first odd number encountered in a list of integers. The method should search the list for the earliest even number and the earliest odd number, then return the product of these two numbers.

-----Input-----  
The input consists of:  
lst: A list of integers.

-----Output-----  
The output is an integer:  
Returns the product resulting from multiplying the first even number and the first odd number found in the list.

-----Note-----  
The input list is assumed to contain at least one even number and one odd number.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux
def isEven (n : Int) : Bool :=
  n % 2 = 0

def isOdd (n : Int) : Bool :=
  n % 2 ≠ 0

def firstEvenOddIndices (lst : List Int) : Option (Nat × Nat) :=
  let evenIndex := lst.findIdx? isEven
  let oddIndex := lst.findIdx? isOdd
  match evenIndex, oddIndex with
  | some ei, some oi => some (ei, oi)
  | _, _ => none
-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def findProduct_precond (lst : List Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  lst.length > 1 ∧
  (∃ x ∈ lst, isEven x) ∧
  (∃ x ∈ lst, isOdd x)
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def findProduct (lst : List Int) (h_precond : findProduct_precond (lst)) : Int :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  match firstEvenOddIndices lst with
  | some (ei, oi) => lst[ei]! * lst[oi]!
  | none => 0
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def findProduct_postcond (lst : List Int) (result: Int) (h_precond : findProduct_precond (lst)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  match firstEvenOddIndices lst with
  | some (ei, oi) => result = lst[ei]! * lst[oi]!
  | none => True
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem findProduct_spec_satisfied (lst: List Int) (h_precond : findProduct_precond (lst)) :
    findProduct_postcond (lst) (findProduct (lst) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold findProduct findProduct_postcond
  split
  case h_1 _ ei oi _ =>
    split
    case h_1 _ ei' oi' heq =>
      have : ei = ei' ∧ oi = oi' := by
        rw [Option.some_inj] at heq
        cases heq with
        | refl => exact ⟨rfl, rfl⟩
      simp [this]
    case h_2 _ heq => contradiction
  case h_2 => simp
  -- !benchmark @end proof

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux
@[reducible, simp]
def reverse_precond (a : Array Int) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux
def reverse_core (arr : Array Int) (i : Nat) : Array Int :=
  if i < arr.size / 2 then
    let j := arr.size - 1 - i
    let temp := arr[i]!
    let arr' := arr.set! i (arr[j]!)
    let arr'' := arr'.set! j temp
    reverse_core arr'' (i + 1)
  else
    arr
-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def reverse (a : Array Int) (h_precond : reverse_precond (a)) : Array Int :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  reverse_core a 0
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def reverse_postcond (a : Array Int) (result: Array Int) (h_precond : reverse_precond (a)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (result.size = a.size) ∧ (∀ i : Nat, i < a.size → result[i]! = a[a.size - 1 - i]!)
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem reverse_spec_satisfied (a: Array Int) (h_precond : reverse_precond (a)) :
    reverse_postcond (a) (reverse (a) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold reverse reverse_postcond
  
  -- Prove a stronger invariant for reverse_core
  have core_inv : ∀ (init arr : Array Int) (i : Nat), 
    arr.size = init.size →
    (∀ k, k < i → arr[k]! = init[init.size - 1 - k]!) →
    (∀ k, k < i → arr[init.size - 1 - k]! = init[k]!) →
    (∀ k, i ≤ k → k < init.size - i → arr[k]! = init[k]!) →
    let res := reverse_core arr i
    res.size = init.size ∧ (∀ k, k < init.size → res[k]! = init[init.size - 1 - k]!) := by
    intro init arr i hsize h1 h2 h3
    induction i using Nat.strongInductionOn with
    | ind i ih =>
      simp only [reverse_core]
      split
      case isFalse h_not_lt =>
        -- Base case: i >= size / 2
        constructor
        · exact hsize
        · intro k hk
          have hk_eq : init.size - 1 - k = k := by omega
          by_cases hk_i : k < i
          · exact h1 k hk_i
          · -- k >= i. We need h3.
            have hk_ub : k < init.size - i := by omega
            have := h3 k (by omega) hk_ub
            rw [hk_eq]
            exact this
      case isTrue h_lt =>
        -- Step case: i < size / 2
        let j := arr.size - 1 - i
        have hj : j < arr.size := by simp [j]; omega
        have hi : i < arr.size := by omega
        let temp := arr[i]!
        let arr' := arr.set! i arr[j]!
        let arr'' := arr'.set! j temp
        
        have h_arr'_size : arr'.size = arr.size := by simp [arr', Array.size_setIfInBounds]
        have h_arr''_size : arr''.size = arr.size := by simp [arr'', Array.size_setIfInBounds]

        -- Prepare invariant hypotheses for recursive call
        have h1' : ∀ k, k < i + 1 → arr''[k]! = init[init.size - 1 - k]! := by
          intro k hk
          rcases Nat.lt_succ_iff_lt_or_eq.mp hk with hk_lt | hk_eq
          · -- k < i
            have h_update : arr''[k]! = arr[k]! := by
               simp only [arr'', arr', Array.getElem_setIfInBounds, hi, hj]
               have h_ne_i : k ≠ i := by omega
               have h_ne_j : k ≠ j := by
                 intro heq; rw [heq] at hk_lt; have : j < i := hk_lt; omega
               simp [h_ne_i, h_ne_j]
            rw [h_update, h1 k hk_lt]
          · -- k = i
            simp only [hk_eq, Array.getElem_setIfInBounds, hi]
            simp only [Array.getElem_setIfInBounds, hj]
            have h_val : arr[j]! = init[init.size - 1 - i]! := by
               have : arr[j]! = init[j]! := h3 j (by omega) (by omega)
               simp [j, hsize] at this; exact this
            exact h_val
            
        have h2' : ∀ k, k < i + 1 → arr''[init.size - 1 - k]! = init[k]! := by
          intro k hk
          rcases Nat.lt_succ_iff_lt_or_eq.mp hk with hk_lt | hk_eq
          · -- k < i
            have idx_ub : init.size - 1 - k < init.size := by omega
            have h_update : arr''[init.size - 1 - k]! = arr[init.size - 1 - k]! := by
               simp only [arr'', arr', Array.getElem_setIfInBounds, hi, hj]
               have h_ne_i : init.size - 1 - k ≠ i := by omega
               have h_ne_j : init.size - 1 - k ≠ j := by omega
               simp [h_ne_i, h_ne_j]
            rw [h_update]
            have := h2 k hk_lt; exact this
          · -- k = i
            simp only [hk_eq, j, hsize]
            simp only [arr'', Array.getElem_setIfInBounds, hj]
            have h_i : arr[i]! = init[i]! := h3 i (by omega) (by omega)
            exact h_i
            
        have h3' : ∀ k, i + 1 ≤ k → k < init.size - (i + 1) → arr''[k]! = init[k]! := by
          intro k hk_lb hk_ub
          have h_update : arr''[k]! = arr[k]! := by
            simp only [arr'', arr', Array.getElem_setIfInBounds, hi, hj]
            have h_ne_i : k ≠ i := by omega
            have h_ne_j : k ≠ j := by
              intro heq
              rw [heq] at hk_ub
              simp [j, hsize] at hk_ub
              omega
            simp [h_ne_i, h_ne_j]
          rw [h_update]
          have h_k_range : i ≤ k ∧ k < init.size - i := by omega
          exact h3 k h_k_range.1 h_k_range.2

        -- Apply IH
        have := ih (i + 1) (by omega) arr'' (by simp [hsize]) h1' h2' h3'
        exact this

  -- Apply the invariant to the initial call
  have h_init : a.size = a.size := rfl
  have h1_init : ∀ k, k < 0 → a[k]! = a[a.size - 1 - k]! := by simp
  have h2_init : ∀ k, k < 0 → a[a.size - 1 - k]! = a[k]! := by simp
  have h3_init : ∀ k, 0 ≤ k → k < a.size - 0 → a[k]! = a[k]! := by simp; intros; rfl
  exact core_inv a a 0 h_init h1_init h2_init h3_init
  -- !benchmark @end proof
```

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description----- 
This task requires writing a Lean 4 method that converts all uppercase characters in a given string to their lowercase equivalents while keeping the other characters unchanged. The output string must have the same length as the input string.

-----Input-----
The input consists of:
s: A string that may contain both uppercase and lowercase characters.

-----Output-----
The output is a string:
Returns a new string where every uppercase letter has been converted to lowercase, and every non-uppercase character remains exactly as in the input.

-----Note-----
There are no preconditions; the method is expected to work for any non-null string.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux
def isUpperCase (c : Char) : Bool :=
  'A' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ 'Z'

def shift32 (c : Char) : Char :=
  Char.ofNat (c.toNat + 32)
-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux

@[reducible, simp]
def toLowercase_precond (s : String) : Prop :=
  -- !benchmark @start precond
  True
  -- !benchmark @end precond


-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def toLowercase (s : String) (h_precond : toLowercase_precond (s)) : String :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let cs := s.toList
  let cs' := cs.map (fun c => if isUpperCase c then shift32 c else c)
  String.mk cs'
  -- !benchmark @end code


-- !benchmark @start postcond_aux

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def toLowercase_postcond (s : String) (result: String) (h_precond : toLowercase_precond (s)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  let cs := s.toList
  let cs' := result.toList
  (result.length = s.length) ∧
  (∀ i : Nat, i < s.length →
    (isUpperCase cs[i]! → cs'[i]! = shift32 cs[i]!) ∧
    (¬isUpperCase cs[i]! → cs'[i]! = cs[i]!))
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem toLowercase_spec_satisfied (s: String) (h_precond : toLowercase_precond (s)) :
    toLowercase_postcond (s) (toLowercase (s) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  unfold toLowercase toLowercase_postcond
  simp_all
  constructor
  · unfold String.length
    simp
  · intro i hi
    have hi' : i < s.data.length := by
      unfold String.length at hi
      simp at hi
      exact hi
    constructor <;> simp_all
  -- !benchmark @end proof

Complete the following Lean 4 proof.

-----Description-----  
Determine whether two strings match based on a specific pattern: for each position in the strings, either the characters are the same, or the character in p is a wildcard represented by a question mark '?' that may match any character.

-----Input-----  
The input consists of:  
• s: A string that is to be matched.  
• p: A pattern string of equal length, where each character is either a specific character or the wildcard '?'.

-----Output-----  
The output is a Boolean value:  
• Returns true if the length of s is equal to the length of p and each corresponding character in s and p are either identical or the character in p is a '?'.  
• Returns false if any character in s does not match the corresponding character in p and the character in p is not a '?'.

-----Note-----  
It is assumed that both strings provided have the same length.

```lean4
-- !benchmark @start import type=solution

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @start solution_aux

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start code_aux

-- !benchmark @end code_aux


def Match (s : String) (p : String) (h_precond : Match_precond (s) (p)) : Bool :=
  -- !benchmark @start code
  let sList := s.toList
  let pList := p.toList
  let rec loop (i : Nat) : Bool :=
    if i < sList.length then
      if (sList[i]! ≠ pList[i]!) ∧ (pList[i]! ≠ '?') then false
      else loop (i + 1)
    else true
  loop 0
  -- !benchmark @end code

-- !benchmark @end postcond_aux


@[reducible, simp]
def Match_postcond (s : String) (p : String) (result: Bool) (h_precond : Match_precond (s) (p)) :=
  -- !benchmark @start postcond
  (result = true ↔ ∀ n : Nat, n < s.toList.length → ((s.toList[n]! = p.toList[n]!) ∨ (p.toList[n]! = '?')))
  -- !benchmark @end postcond


-- !benchmark @start proof_aux

-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem Match_spec_satisfied (s: String) (p: String) (h_precond : Match_precond (s) (p)) :
    Match_postcond (s) (p) (Match (s) (p) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof

```lean4

-- !benchmark @end import

-- !benchmark @end solution_aux

-- !benchmark @start precond_aux

-- !benchmark @end precond_aux
@[reducible, simp]
